title: Vardalux _draft.json
description: Pipeline state checkpoint. Written progressively across steps 0-4. Each Python tool validates against this schema before reading or writing. Step sequence: 0=WatchTrack, 1=Photos, 2=Pricing, 3=Descriptions, 3.5=Grailzee gate, 4=PDF generated.

step.description: Last completed pipeline step. 3.5 = Grailzee gate resolved.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime of last write to _draft.json.

inputs.description: Watch data collected across steps 0-1. All fields optional — populated progressively. additionalProperties: true so watch-specific fields (dial_color, bezel_material, etc.) are allowed without schema changes.

internal_ref.description: First segment of folder name, e.g. '164WU'.

model_ref.description: Remaining segments of folder name, e.g. 'IW371446-1'.

retail_net.description: Target NET after all fees and negotiation.

wta_comp.description: Lowest US dealer comp from C24 or eBay.

condition.description: Canonical condition. Normalization rule: WatchTrack 'Brand New' maps to BNIB and is written at step 0. All other WatchTrack condition strings require user confirmation via Telegram buttons — the field is left absent from the draft until the user confirms.

condition_detail.description: Component-level notes (case, bezel, crystal, dial, movement, bracelet, crown). Required when condition is Other or when Chrono24 Condition Notes section is needed.

grailzee_format.description: NR = no-reserve $1 start, Reserve = user-provided reserve price, skip = exclude from listing.

buffer.description: Negotiation buffer percentage added to retail_net before fee calculation. Default: 5.

included.description: What ships with the watch, e.g. 'Box and papers'.

inputs_pending.description: Staging area for inputs received before step 1 confirmation (e.g., retail_net texted during WatchTrack review). Merged into inputs at the step 1 approval gate and then removed. Nulled out after merge.

watchtrack.description: Data extracted from WatchTrack at step 0. additionalProperties: true — WatchTrack returns extra fields (sub_status, sourced_from, received, retail_price_wt, etc.) that are preserved verbatim.

notes.description: Verbatim WatchTrack Notes field. Always captured regardless of whether price fields are populated.

recent_comps.description: Sold comps. Either a plain array of prices (spec format) or a WatchTrack-native structured object with source, ranges, and counts.

oneOf.description: WatchTrack structured comp object with source, global_count, us_range, etc.

pricing.description: Platform pricing calculated by run_pricing.py at step 2. Absent until step 2 completes.

facebook_wholesale.description: Null when wholesale_net was not provided.

wta.description: Null when wta_price was not provided.

status.description: OK = at or below sweet_spot, NOTE = compliant but above sweet_spot, OVER = exceeds max_allowed.

reddit.description: Null when reddit_price was not provided.

reserve_price.description: Required when format is Reserve. Null for NR.

canonical.description: Descriptions written by the step3a-canonical skill. Absent until step 3 completes.

grailzee_desc.description: Emotional paragraph, no specs, no condition. Null when grailzee_format is skip.

approved.description: Human approval gates. Each sub-object records the outcome of a Telegram approval interaction.

table.description: Snapshot of the pricing table at approval time.
